title: Subqueries, CTEs, Set Operations, and Relational Reasoning · Changed-Data Lab
summary: Operations needs reusable query stages for backlog comparisons, missing relationships, and cohorts across time windows. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a composable backlog-analysis query pipeline with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose scalar, row, and table subqueries whose cardinality matches their surrounding expression.
Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions.
Use CTEs to name query stages while distinguishing readability from materialization guarantees.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-set-operations-1sjci6
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
WITH unresolved AS (
  SELECT ticket_id, team_id FROM tickets WHERE status <> 'resolved'
)
SELECT team_id, COUNT(*) AS unresolved_count
FROM unresolved GROUP BY team_id ORDER BY team_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
